methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series E → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.4x) to $2.8B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 3 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Space & Aerospace. Range: $1.0B - $3.5B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Space & Aerospace P/S median is 53.2x across 6 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

data.narrative:
ICEYE is a Series E space & aerospace company with a current post-money valuation of $2.8B, having raised $650M+ to date. The valuation analysis triangulates against a comp universe of 5 public peers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL) and 8 private companies in the same sector and stage.

Two methods yielded actionable guidance. Stage-progression analysis applies a 1.4x step-up multiplier to the $2.8B Series E post-money, implying a forward valuation of $3.9B. Peer-median triangulation across three same-sector, same/adjacent-stage comparables—Astranis ($3.5B Series D), Apex Space ($1.0B Series D), and Stoke Space ($1.0B Series D)—yields a median of $1.0B, with a range of $1.0B to $3.5B. Public-comp anchoring proved inapplicable due to absence of target revenue disclosure, though the Space & Aerospace sector median trades at 53.2x revenue across 6 public tickers.

The two applicable methods support a triangulated range of $850M (low) to $4.5B (high), with a median of $2.5B. The current $2.8B valuation sits comfortably within this range, closer to the median. Key uncertainty stems from the wide dispersion in peer valuations ($1.0B–$3.5B), reflecting heterogeneous revenue scales and growth trajectories within the sector. Revenue materialization and customer concentration merit scrutiny given the stage.
